Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund: Everything You Need to Know
Section Title: ... > A mutual fund with a focus on Taiwan’s economy, including exports, electronics manufacturin...
Content:
Written by [Equitymaster](https://www.financialexpress.com/author/equitymaster/)
April 9, 2026 12:06 IST
[](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=financialexpress.com)
 ... 
Section Title: ... > A mutual fund with a focus on Taiwan’s economy, including exports, electronics manufacturin...
Content:
[Follow Us](https://news.google.com/publications/CAAqBwgKMOeb3gow873VAQ?hl=en-IN&gl=IN&ceid=IN%3Aen)
Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund: Everything You Need to Know (Image: AI-Generated)
As global supply chains shift and the “ [China+1](https://www.equitymaster.com/detail.asp?ref=articlehyperlinking) ” strategy gains traction, [Taiwan](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/taiwan/?ref=articlehyperlinking) has emerged as a critical hub in the technology and [semiconductor ecosystem](https://www.equitymaster.com/detail.asp?ref=articlehyperlinking) .
While Indian stocks have delivered negative returns, Taiwanese stocks have delivered positive returns, with the Taiwan Capitalization-Weighted Stock Index (TAIEX) delivering 53% over the last year.
Section Title: ... > A mutual fund with a focus on Taiwan’s economy, including exports, electronics manufacturin...
Content:
The [Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund](https://www.financialexpress.com/mutual-funds/nippon-india-taiwan-equity-fund-regular-plan-idcw-INF204KC1287/?ref=articlehyperlinking) provides exposure to this segment by investing predominantly in Taiwanese equities, with a portfolio built around industry leaders.
ALSO READ
[GRSE vs Mazagon Dock: Which Shipbuilding Stock is Better?](https://www.financialexpress.com/market/stock-insights/grse-vs-mazagon-dock-which-shipbuilding-stock-is-better/4199841/)
The fund combines a high-conviction approach with selective diversification, aiming to capture Taiwan’s export-led growth and innovation ecosystem.
Let’s take a closer look at the fund…
Section Title: ... > **About the Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund**
Content:
The [Nippon India](https://www.financialexpress.com/mutual-funds/amc/nippon-india-mutual-fund/?ref=articlehyperlinking) Taiwan Equity Fund was launched on 11 December 2021. It is an open-ended [equity](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/equity-funds/?ref=articlehyperlinking) scheme following a Taiwan-focused theme.
As of 28 February 2026, the fund holds a month-end Asset Under Management (AUM) of Rs 5.2 bn. The expense ratio of the direct plan is 0.98%.
It’s benchmarked against the TAIEX Total Return Index (TRI), which measures the aggregate of listed stocks on the Taiwan Stock Exchange.
ALSO READ
[5 Penny Stocks that Have Rallied in 2026. Will the Momentum Continue?](https://www.financialexpress.com/market/stock-insights-5-penny-stocks-that-have-rallied-in-2026-will-the-momentum-continue-4196876/)
 ... 
Section Title: Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund: Everything You Need to Know > **Asset Allocation**
Content:
Under normal market conditions, the scheme aims to allocate 80-100% of its total [assets](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/asset-allocation/?ref=articlehyperlinking) to Taiwanese equities and 0-20% to debt and money market instruments. The fund’s [debt](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/debt/?ref=articlehyperlinking) allocation is primarily used for cash management.
As of 28 February 2026, the fund’s asset allocation stood at 86.17% in equities and 13.83% in cash and other receivables.
ALSO READ
[Should You Invest in Dividend Yield Funds?](https://www.financialexpress.com/money/insights/should-you-invest-in-dividend-yield-funds/4192817/)
Section Title: Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund: Everything You Need to Know > **Portfolio Positioning**
Content:
The [portfolio](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/investment-portfolio/?ref=articlehyperlinking) is structured to capitalise on Taiwan’s leadership position in the global semiconductor industry and its strong science and technology infrastructure.
In sector allocation, semiconductors account for the largest share (21.49%), followed by [semiconductor](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/semiconductor/?ref=articlehyperlinking) materials and equipment (18.72%), [electronic](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/electronics/?ref=articlehyperlinking) components (14.31%), and [technology](https://www.financialexpress.com/about/business-technology/?ref=articlehyperlinking) hardware, storage, and peripherals (10.46%).
 ... 
Section Title: Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund: Everything You Need to Know > **Conclusion**
Content:
The Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund stands out for its tightly focused international strategy built around Taiwan’s role in the global technology and semiconductor supply chain.
Its portfolio construction reflects this clearly, with high conviction and limited diversification across just 26 stocks.
The exposure is narrow, both in terms of country and sector, which increases dependence on Taiwan’s tech cycle and global semiconductor demand.
This positioning has translated into strong performance over the past two years, with the fund performing well ahead of its benchmark.

Section Title: ... > ### About Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund Direct Growth
Content:
Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by Nippon India Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 30 Jun 1995. Kinjal Desai is the Current Fund Manager of Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹7,41,774 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 02 Apr 2026 is ₹26.61.
The Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹500. Exit load of 1% if redeemed within 3 months.
;
Section Title: Nippon India Taiwan Equity Fund Direct Growth > ... > Investment Objective
Content:
The scheme seeks to provide long term capital appreciation to investors by primarily investing in equity and equity related securities of companies listed on the recognized stock exchanges of Taiwan and the secondary objective is to generate consistent returns by investing in debt and money market securities of India.;
Fund benchmark Taiwan Capitalization Weighted Stock Index
[Scheme Information Document(SID)](https://mf.nipponindiaim.com/)
